_comment: Configuration matrix for the native-vs-wasm benchmark. Each config runs the SAME workloads (workloads.json) so cells are directly comparable. The wasm configs vary along two axes: AOT (compile-at-instantiation .wasm vs wasmtime-precompiled .cwasm) and EMBEDDED (the extension dynamically loaded + host-dispatched, vs compiled INTO the core as a native scalar with no cross-component WIT dispatch). 'native' is the baseline. Artifact paths are resolved per config from the artifact roots passed via env (see run_matrix.py). A config whose artifacts are absent is reported as 'skipped' (not an error) so a partial matrix is still publishable.

configs.title: NATIVE (bundled DuckDB + native ducklink ext)
configs.note: Baseline. Native engine builds DataChunks; reg_duckdb marshals across WIT into the component (embedded wasmtime). The extension algorithm is still wasm.

configs.title: WASM DYNAMIC, no-AOT
configs.note: Core + extension as separate components, host-resolver/WIT-dispatched, Cranelift-compiled at instantiation. The current shipped wasm path.

configs.title: WASM DYNAMIC, AOT
configs.note: Same dynamic dispatch, but core + cli loaded from wasmtime-precompiled .cwasm (deserialize, no compile). Isolates the one-time compile cost from steady-state throughput.

configs.title: WASM EMBEDDED, no-AOT
configs.note: Extension compiled INTO the core (embed framework: registered as a native scalar, no cross-component WIT dispatch boundary), compiled at instantiation.

configs.title: WASM EMBEDDED, AOT
configs.note: Embedded + precompiled .cwasm. The expected near-native upper bound once the columnar @4.0.0 ABI lands.
